summary: Russian forces intensified drone attacks across multiple fronts, striking residential areas in Odesa and Kharkiv and targeting critical infrastructure such as the Kuibyshev refinery. Ukrainian defenses around Kostiantynivka are reported as collapsing, with Russian assault groups penetrating the city center from several directions. Additional threats include ballistic missiles, KAB rockets, and Shahed‑136 drones launched from various regions.
key_points: Mass drone attacks hit Kharkiv, injuring at least six people and causing a residential building fire in the Industrial district (Source: @UkraineNow/81820).
Two UAVs were observed over Kharkiv near Derhachi, indicating continued aerial pressure on the city (Source: @eRadarrua/96179).
Russian troops broke through Kostiantynivka’s defenses, advancing from Illinovka and Berestok through key city landmarks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30804).
A drone from the Black Sea was tracked heading toward Odesa and Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/64764).
Ukrainian Shahed‑136 type strike drones were launched from the Millerevo area (Source: @eRadarrua/96150).
The Kuibyshev oil refinery halted operations after a reported Ukrainian drone strike (Source: @eRadarrua/96161).
Ballistic weapon threats were announced for front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/64733).

sections.executive_summary: Mass drone attacks hit Kharkiv, injuring at least six people and causing a residential building fire in the Industrial district. Russian troops broke through Kostiantynivka’s defenses, advancing from multiple directions into the city center. Additional aerial threats include Shahed‑136 drones, KAB rockets, and ballistic weapons across several fronts.

points: In Kharkiv, a mass drone assault resulted in at least six civilian injuries and ignited a fire in a residential building within the Industrial district, prompting emergency response teams (Source: @UkraineNow/81820).
Ukrainian forces reported the presence of two UAVs over Kharkiv near Derhachi, indicating ongoing low‑altitude threats to the city’s outskirts (Source: @eRadarrua/96179).

Five drones were observed over Prosyana in the south, and additional UAV activity was noted in Kirovohrad, Zaporizhzhia (Tomakivka), and Kharkiv (Prolisne), showing a broadening aerial campaign (Source: @eRadarrua/96184).
A Shahed‑136 type strike drone launch was detected originating from the Millerevo area, highlighting the use of loitering munitions against Ukrainian targets (Source: @eRadarrua/96150).
The Kuibyshev oil refinery ceased operations after a Ukrainian drone attack, demonstrating the impact of aerial strikes on critical energy infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/96161).
Sochi airport experienced prolonged circling of an aircraft from Sharm‑El‑Sheikh due to a denied landing caused by a drone threat, reflecting regional airspace disruptions (Source: @eRadarrua/96129).

Russian troops achieved breakthroughs in Kostiantynivka, entering the city center from Illinovka and Berestok and moving through the 7 Vetrov shopping centre and Yubileiny Park toward Yemelyanova Street and the Krivoy Torets River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30804).
A drone originating from the Black Sea was tracked heading toward Odesa and the port city of Chornomorsk, indicating Russian use of maritime UAV routes (Source: @kpszsu/64764).
Russian forces announced the deployment of KAB ballistic missiles toward southeastern Kharkiv and Donetsk regions, expanding long‑range strike capabilities (Source: @kpszsu/64754).
Threats of ballistic weapon use were publicly communicated for front‑line areas, suggesting preparation for high‑impact strikes (Source: @kpszsu/64733).
A hostile tactical aviation presence was reported on the north‑east and southern axes, warning of possible air‑to‑ground attacks near front‑line zones (Source: @kpszsu/64744).

In Odesa’s Primorsky district, Russian attacks damaged two residential buildings, completely destroying a fifth‑floor apartment and injuring a 46‑year‑old woman who was hospitalized (Source: @UkraineNow/81811).
Ukrainian defenses around Kostiantynivka are collapsing as Russian assault groups penetrate multiple city sectors, including key commercial and park areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30804).
Drone activity was reported east of Chernihiv, with UAVs heading toward Sosnytsia, indicating pressure on northern front‑line positions (Source: @kpszsu/64745).
Shahed‑136 loitering munitions were launched from Millerevo, targeting eastern operational zones (Source: @eRadarrua/96150).
A drone was observed heading east of Kharkiv toward Izium, suggesting continued Russian aerial focus on contested eastern districts (Source: @kpszsu/64765).
Five UAVs over Prosyana (south) and additional sightings in Zaporizhzhia and Kirovohrad reflect a dispersed eastern aerial threat pattern (Source: @eRadarrua/96184).
Ukrainian reports indicate a drone from the Black Sea moving toward Odesa and Chornomorsk, affecting northern coastal defenses (Source: @kpszsu/64764).
Hostile tactical aviation activity was warned for the north‑east direction, raising concerns of air strikes near front‑line areas (Source: @kpszsu/64744).
Drone sightings in Sumy region near Vorozhba, heading southwest, suggest Russian UAV operations near the northern border (Source: @kpszsu/64756).
Ukrainian forces observed UAVs over Kharkiv Derhachi, a city close to the northern front, indicating ongoing aerial pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/96179).
Five drones were reported over Prosyana in the south, indicating heightened UAV activity on the southern axis (Source: @eRadarrua/96184).
KAB missile threats were announced for the southeastern Kharkiv and Donetsk areas, extending long‑range strike capability toward the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/64754).
A drone from the Black Sea heading toward Odesa and Chornomorsk poses a maritime‑air threat to southern coastal infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/64764).
Ballistic weapon threats were communicated for front‑line zones, potentially affecting southern operational areas (Source: @kpszsu/64733).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on north‑east and southern directions, warning of possible air‑to‑ground attacks near front‑line regions (Source: @kpszsu/64744).
KAB ballistic missiles were announced for southeastern Kharkiv and Donetsk, indicating an escalation in long‑range aerial strike options (Source: @kpszsu/64754).
Shahed‑136 loitering munitions were launched from Millerevo, adding a new dimension to the UAV threat landscape (Source: @eRadarrua/96150).
A drone from the Black Sea was tracked heading toward Odesa and Chornomorsk, highlighting maritime‑air integration in Russian operations (Source: @kpszsu/64764).
Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions over Kharkiv, Derhachi, and Prolisne, demonstrating persistent low‑altitude pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/96179, @eRadarrua/96184).

key_developments: Mass drone attacks struck Kharkiv, injuring at least six civilians and igniting a residential building fire in the Industrial district (Source: @UkraineNow/81820).
Russian troops broke through Kostiantynivka’s defenses, advancing from Illinovka and Berestok through key city landmarks into the center (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30804).
A drone from the Black Sea was tracked heading toward Odesa and Chornomorsk, indicating maritime UAV routes used by Russian forces (Source: @kpszsu/64764).
Shahed‑136 type loitering munitions were launched from the Millerevo area, expanding the UAV threat against Ukrainian positions (Source: @eRadarrua/96150).
KAB ballistic missiles were announced for southeastern Kharkiv and Donetsk, showing an escalation in long‑range strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/64754).
The Kuibyshev oil refinery halted operations after a Ukrainian drone strike, highlighting the impact on critical energy infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/96161).
Ballistic weapon threats were publicly communicated for front‑line areas, suggesting preparation for high‑impact attacks (Source: @kpszsu/64733).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are intensifying multi‑axis UAV operations, employing both loitering munitions and conventional drones to target urban centers, infrastructure, and front‑line positions. The breakthrough in Kostiantynivka signals a potential shift in ground dynamics on the eastern front, while the introduction of KAB ballistic missiles and announced ballistic weapon use raises the risk of high‑payload strikes across the southern and eastern sectors. Ukrainian defenses face compounded pressure from aerial, missile, and ground assaults, demanding rapid adaptation and resource allocation.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have mobilized emergency services in Odesa and Kharkiv, evacuating civilians and reinforcing air‑defense assets after repeated drone incursions. International partners are reportedly supplying additional air‑defense systems, though specific deployments were not detailed in the source material. The ongoing attacks have prompted heightened alert levels across border regions, with local law‑enforcement responding to incidents involving armed groups and civil unrest.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths from the Black Sea and Millerevo should be prioritized to anticipate future strike vectors. The collapse of defensive lines in Kostiantynivka warrants close observation for possible Russian exploitation of supply routes and further urban penetration. The emergence of KAB missiles and ballistic weapon threats indicates a diversification of Russian strike capabilities, necessitating updated threat models for both ground and air assets.
